articles.headline: Angel Reese has 15 points and 17 rebounds, including 11 offensive, and Dream beat Tempo 102-77
articles.description: — Angel Reese had 15 points and 17 rebounds — which included a career-high 11 offensive, one short of tying the WNBA single-game record — and the Atlanta Dream beat the Toronto Tempo 102-77 on Sunday.

images.name: Toronto Tempo vs. Atlanta Dream - Game Highlights
images.caption: Watch the Game Highlights from Toronto Tempo vs. Atlanta Dream, 06/14/2026

articles.headline: Liberty win 7th straight, clinch spot in Commissioner's Cup final
articles.description: The Liberty beat the Mystics 86-64 on Sunday and clinched a spot in the Commissioner's Cup championship game.

images.name: Breanna Stewart (June 14, 2026) [1296x729]
images.caption: The Liberty's Breanna Stewart on Sunday became only the second player in WNBA history to have at least 10 points, 10 rebounds and five blocks in a half. She finished the game with 14 points, 12 rebounds and a career-high seven blocks.

articles.headline: Liberty clinch spot in Commissioner's Cup finals with win over Mystics, extend win streak to 7
articles.description: — Breanna Stewart had 14 points, 12 rebounds and a career-high seven blocks to help the New York Liberty beat the Washington Mystics 86-64 on Sunday and clinch a spot in the Commissioner's Cup championship game.

articles.headline: Kelsey Plum, Kahleah Copper author dueling 40-point games
articles.description: Sparks guard Kelsey Plum had 43 points and Mercury guard Kahleah Copper finished with 41, the first time opponents have had dueling 40-point games in WNBA history.

articles.headline: Aces tighten grip on West lead in Commissioner's Cup with win over Lynx
articles.description: The Aces moved closer to their third appearance in the WNBA's Commissioner's Cup final with a 100-97 victory over the Lynx.

articles.headline: WNBA players with the most points in a single game
articles.description: A'ja Wilson and Liz Cambage have notched the most points in a single WNBA game. Find out which other players make the list.

articles.headline: Kelsey Plum scores a career-high 43 points in the Sparks' OT win over the Mercury
articles.description: — Kelsey Plum scored a career-high 43 points, offsetting Kahleah Copper's career-best 41-point effort and the Los Angeles Sparks beat the Phoenix Mercury 111-102 in overtime Saturday night to improve to 3-2 in Commissioner's Cup play.

articles.headline: Fire beat the Wings 84-83 to end a 4-game losing streak
articles.description: — Bridget Carleton scored 20 points, Emily Engstler made the second of two free throws with two seconds left and the Portland Fire beat the Dallas Wings 84-83 on Saturday night in Commissioner's Cup play to end a four-game losing streak.

article.headline: Carter scores 22, Young adds 20 as Aces beat Sparks 105-78
article.description: — Chennedy Carter scored 22 points, Jackie Young had 20 points and nine assists, and the Las Vegas Aces beat Los Angeles 105-78 on Sunday in the Sparks' season opener.
article.linkText: Carter scores 22, Young adds 20 as Aces beat Sparks 105-78

categories.description: Las Vegas Aces @ Los Angeles Sparks

event.description: Las Vegas Aces @ Los Angeles Sparks

tracking.trackingName: WNBA_One-Play (Kelsey Plum hits the basket) 2026/05/10 ESHEET

categories.description: Las Vegas Aces @ Los Angeles Sparks

event.description: Las Vegas Aces @ Los Angeles Sparks

keywords: Las Vegas Aces @ Los Angeles Sparks
article.story: LOS ANGELES -- — Chennedy Carter scored 22 points, Jackie Young had 20 points and nine assists, and the Las Vegas Aces beat Los Angeles 105-78 on Sunday in the Sparks' season opener. A’ja Wilson had 19 points and four rebounds for Las Vegas. Wilson has 2,502 career rebounds in 269 games, tied with Lisa Leslie for third fastest to reach 2,500 in WNBA history. Tina Charles reached milestone in a record 248 games. Chelsea Gray scored 16 points and NaLyssa Smith 12. Las Vegas, the defending WNBA champion, lost 99-66 to Phoenix in its season opener Saturday. Against Los Angeles, the Aces shot 62% (43 of 69) from the field — the second-best mark in franchise history. Las Vegas scored the final 14 points over the final 3 1/2 minutes in the first quarter to take a 29-14 lead into the second. The Sparks were 0-for-6 shooting and committed three turnovers during that span. The Sparks scored 19 — nine by Kelsey Plum — of the final 24 points in the first half to trim their deficit to 42-41 at halftime, but the Aces opened the third quarter with a 12-4 spurt and outscored LA 33-18 in the period. Carter's layup with 4:21 left made it 61-51 and Las Vegas led by double figures the rest of the way. Plum led the Sparks with 27 points. Nneka Ogwumike, who spent her first 12 seasons with Los Angeles, returned to the Sparks after two seasons with Seattle and had 19 points and 10 rebounds. Up next Aces: Play Wednesday and again Friday at Connecticut. Sparks: Host Indiana on Wednesday. ------ AP WNBA: https://apnews.com/hub/wnba-basketball
